text: Multi-factor authentication is required for privileged and remote access.
Access to systems is provisioned based on least privilege and role-based access control.
User access reviews are conducted at least annually.
Sensitive customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 or equivalent.
Data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
Security-relevant events are logged and retained for at least 90 days.
Logs are protected from unauthorized modification or deletion.
Vulnerability scans are conducted at least quarterly on all systems.
Critical vulnerabilities are remediated within 30 days of discovery.
A formal incident response plan is documented and tested annually.
Security incidents are investigated contained and documented.
Changes to production systems require approval and testing prior to deployment.
Background checks are performed on all employees with access to sensitive data.
Security awareness training is provided to all employees at least annually.
Physical access to data centers is restricted and monitored.
Business continuity and disaster recovery plans are documented and tested.
Third-party vendors are assessed for security risk prior to engagement.
A data classification policy defines handling requirements for sensitive data.
Network access is controlled via firewalls segmentation and intrusion detection.
Applications undergo security testing (SAST/DAST/penetration testing) before release.
